examples.title: Generic Project Management Life Cycle Flow
examples.description: Pseudocode representing the flow through all phases of the Project Management Life Cycle
examples.code:
PROJECT LifeCycle()
BEGIN
 // Phase 1: Initiation
 projectConcept = DEFINE_CONCEPT()
 feasibility = CONDUCT_FEASIBILITY(projectConcept)
 IF feasibility == TRUE THEN
 charter = CREATE_PROJECT_CHARTER(projectConcept)
 stakeholders = IDENTIFY_STAKEHOLDERS(projectConcept)
 AUTHORIZE_PROJECT(charter)
 
 // Phase 2: Planning
 IF charter.authorized THEN
 scope = DEFINE_SCOPE(projectConcept)
 WBS = CREATE_WBS(scope)
 schedule = CREATE_SCHEDULE(WBS)
 budget = CREATE_BUDGET(WBS)
 riskPlan = PLAN_RISKS(projectConcept)
 projectPlan = COMBINE_PLANS(scope, WBS, schedule, budget, riskPlan)
 
 // Phase 3: Execution
 WHILE NOT projectPlan.completed DO
 deliverables = CREATE_DELIVERABLES(projectPlan)
 COORDINATE_TEAM(deliverables)
 MANAGE_STAKEHOLDER_COMMUNICATION()
 END WHILE
 
 // Phase 4: Monitoring & Controlling
 WHILE projectPlan.active DO
 performance = MEASURE_PERFORMANCE(projectPlan)
 variance = COMPARE_TO_BASELINE(performance)
 IF variance EXISTS THEN
 IMPLEMENT_CORRECTIVE_ACTION(variance)
 END IF
 MANAGE_CHANGES(projectPlan)
 END WHILE
 
 // Phase 5: Closure
 DELIVER_FINAL_PRODUCT()
 OBTAIN_FORMAL_ACCEPTANCE()
 COMPLETE_DOCUMENTATION()
 RELEASE_RESOURCES()
 CONDUCT_POST_PROJECT_REVIEW()
 CELEBRATE_SUCCESS()
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the flow through all five phases of the Project Management Life Cycle. It shows decision points (feasibility, authorization), iterative processes (execution, monitoring), and final activities (closure). Each phase builds upon the previous one, creating a structured approach to project management.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of project tasks

examples.title: WBS Creation Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm for creating a Work Breakdown Structure
examples.code:
CREATE_WBS(projectScope)
BEGIN
 deliverables = []
 
 // Step 1: Identify Major Deliverables
 majorDeliverables = IDENTIFY_MAJOR_DELIVERABLES(projectScope)
 
 FOR each major IN majorDeliverables DO
 level1 = major
 subDeliverables = []
 
 // Step 2: Decompose each major deliverable
 components = DECOMPOSE(major)
 
 FOR each component IN components DO
 level2 = component
 workPackages = []
 
 // Step 3: Decompose to work packages
 IF IS_DECOMPOSABLE(component) THEN
 workPackages = FURTHER_DECOMPOSE(component)
 ELSE
 workPackages = component
 END IF
 
 // Step 4: Assign unique codes
 FOR each wp IN workPackages DO
 wp.code = ASSIGN_CODE(level1, level2, wp)
 wp.description = ADD_DESCRIPTION(wp)
 wp.estimatedDuration = ESTIMATE_EFFORT(wp)
 wp.assignedTo = ASSIGN_RESOURCE(wp)
 END FOR
 END FOR
 END FOR
 
 // Step 5: Validate WBS
 IF NOT VALIDATE_COMPLETENESS(deliverables, projectScope) THEN
 REVISIT_MAJOR_DELIVERABLES()
 END IF
 
 RETURN WBS
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm shows how to systematically decompose a project scope into a hierarchical Work Breakdown Structure. It starts with major deliverables, breaks them into components, and finally creates work packages at the lowest level with unique codes and assignments.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n log n) for n deliverables

examples.title: Risk Management Process
examples.description: Algorithm representing the risk management process throughout the project
examples.code:
RISK_MANAGEMENT(projectPlan)
BEGIN
 riskRegister = []
 
 // Risk Identification
 risks = IDENTIFY_RISKS(projectPlan)
 
 FOR each risk IN risks DO
 riskEvent = risk.description
 probability = ASSESS_PROBABILITY(riskEvent)
 impact = ASSESS_IMPACT(riskEvent)
 riskScore = probability * impact
 risk.rating = CALCULATE_RATING(riskScore)
 
 ADD_TO_REGISTER(riskRegister, risk)
 END FOR
 
 // Risk Response Planning
 FOR each risk IN riskRegister DO
 IF risk.rating == HIGH THEN
 IF CAN_AVOID(risk) THEN
 risk.response = AVOID
 ELSE IF CAN_TRANSFER(risk) THEN
 risk.response = TRANSFER
 ELSE IF CAN_MITIGATE(risk) THEN
 risk.response = MITIGATE
 ELSE
 risk.response = ACCEPT
 END IF
 ELSE
 risk.response = ACCEPT
 END IF
 
 risk.contingencyPlan = CREATE_CONTINGENCY(risk)
 END FOR
 
 // Risk Monitoring (continuous throughout project)
 WHILE projectPlan.active DO
 triggerOccurred = CHECK_TRIGGERS(riskRegister)
 IF triggerOccurred THEN
 ACTIVATE_RESPONSE(triggeredRisk)
 UPDATE_RISK_REGISTER(riskRegister)
 END IF
 END WHILE
 
 RETURN riskRegister
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the continuous risk management process: identifying risks, analyzing their probability and impact, planning appropriate responses (avoid, transfer, mitigate, accept), and monitoring throughout the project lifecycle.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for initial identification, ongoing O(1) checks
